response_body_utf8: {"status":"ok","message-type":"work","message-version":"1.0.0","message":{"indexed":{"date-parts":[[2026,3,23]],"date-time":"2026-03-23T13:21:35Z","timestamp":1774272095185,"version":"3.50.1"},"reference-count":23,"publisher":"MDPI AG","issue":"6","license":[{"start":{"date-parts":[[2026,3,23]],"date-time":"2026-03-23T00:00:00Z","timestamp":1774224000000},"content-version":"vor","delay-in-days":0,"URL":"https:\/\/creativecommons.org\/licenses\/by\/4.0\/"}],"content-domain":{"domain":[],"crossmark-restriction":false},"short-container-title":["JCM"],"abstract":"<jats:p>Background: Lipomas are common benign subcutaneous neoplasms treated surgically for cosmetic or symptomatic reasons. The minimal one-third incision and four-step (MOTIF) technique provides reliable excision with minimal scarring, but smaller proportional incisions remain unstudied. This study evaluates the minimal one-quarter incision and four-step (MOQIF) technique. Methods: Retrospective review of 82 patients undergoing MOQIF excision of histologically confirmed subcutaneous lipomas by a single surgeon from July 2024\u2013December 2025 was done. Lipomas were stratified by maximum diameter: small-intermediate (<5 cm) and large (\u22655 cm). MOQIF used a one-quarter incision of the lipoma\u2019s long axis determined by preoperative ultrasound measurement and palpation with four steps: hydro dissection preserving superficial subcutaneous tissue, superficial dissection, staged deep dissection with selective cautery of fibrovascular septa, and intact mass delivery. Outcomes included excision length, postoperative complications, Vancouver Scar Scale (VSS) scores, recurrence, and subjective treatment satisfaction of patients. Results: Mean lipoma size was 6.8 \u00b1 2.0 cm (75.6% \u22655 cm). All lipomas were completely excised through 1.69 \u00b1 0.49 cm incisions (ratio 0.25). Complications were low: seroma 10.98% (16.7% vs. 9.4%, p = 0.404), hematoma 7.3% (11.1% vs. 6.3%, p = 0.608), with no infections, nerve injuries, or recurrences at a mean 8.9-month follow-up. VSS scores were equivalent between groups (0.83 vs. 1.06; p = 0.438) and overall patient satisfaction was high (3.54 \u00b1 0.53 (2\u20134)). Conclusions: MOQIF achieves complete lipoma excision through one-quarter incisions with safety and cosmetic outcomes across lipoma sizes, demonstrating feasibility through standardized technique refinement and careful case selection.<\/jats:p>","DOI":"10.3390\/jcm15062448","type":"journal-article","created":{"date-parts":[[2026,3,23]],"date-time":"2026-03-23T11:59:36Z","timestamp":1774267176000},"page":"2448","update-policy":"https:\/\/doi.org\/10.3390\/mdpi_crossmark_policy","source":"Crossref","is-referenced-by-count":0,"title":["Minimal One-Quarter Incision and Four-Step (MOQIF) Excision Method for Subcutaneous Lipoma"],"prefix":"10.3390","volume":"15","author":[{"ORCID":"https:\/\/orcid.org\/0009-0002-9474-7289","authenticated-orcid":false,"given":"Seung Yun","family":"Oh","sequence":"first","affiliation":[{"name":"Faculty of Medicine, Dentistry and Health Sciences, The University of Melbourne, Parkville, Melbourne, VIC 3010, Australia"}],"role":[{"role":"author","vocabulary":"crossref"}]},{"ORCID":"https:\/\/orcid.org\/0000-0003-4372-6955","authenticated-orcid":false,"given":"Seokchan","family":"Eun","sequence":"additional","affiliation":[{"name":"Department of Plastic and Reconstructive Surgery, Seoul National University College of Medicine, Seoul National University Bundang Hospital, 82 Gumi-ro 173beon-gil, Bundang-gu, Seongnam 463-707, Republic of Korea"}],"role":[{"role":"author","vocabulary":"crossref"}]}],"member":"1968","published-online":{"date-parts":[[2026,3,23]]},"reference":[{"key":"ref_1","doi-asserted-by":"crossref","first-page":"562","DOI":"10.5021\/ad.2021.33.6.562","article-title":"The Study of Relationship between Anatomical Sites and Depth of the Lipoma","volume":"33","author":"Kim","year":"2021","journal-title":"Ann.
